# Zefer — AI/LLM Context

> Client-side encryption tool. Encrypts text and files into password-protected .zefer files using AES-256-GCM. 100% browser-based. Open source. MIT License.

## What is Zefer?

Zefer is a web application that allows users to encrypt secrets (text, passwords, API keys, configuration files, images, ZIPs, or any file type) into `.zefer` files that can only be decrypted with the correct passphrase. All encryption and decryption happens in the user's browser using the Web Crypto API. No data ever touches a server.

## How it works

1. User enters text or selects a file
2. User sets a passphrase (minimum 6 characters)
3. PBKDF2-SHA256 derives a 256-bit key (300k-1M iterations, random 32-byte salt)
4. AES-256-GCM encrypts the content (random 12-byte IV)
5. Result is packaged as a .zefer file and downloaded
6. Recipient uploads the .zefer file and enters the passphrase to decrypt

## Security features

- AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption
- PBKDF2-SHA256 key derivation (configurable: 300k, 600k, 1M iterations)
- Reveal key (secondary passphrase for sharing without exposing main key)
- Dual passphrase (requires two keys from different people)
- Secret question with PBKDF2-hashed answer (100k iterations)
- IP restriction (IPv4/IPv6 allowlist, encrypted inside payload)
- Time-based expiration (30min to 2 weeks, or no expiration)
- Maximum decryption attempts (client-side enforcement)
- Gzip/Deflate compression
- Dynamic file size limits based on device capabilities

## Technical details

- Framework: Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript 5, Tailwind CSS v4
- Crypto: Web Crypto API (SubtleCrypto) — hardware-accelerated
- File format: ZEFER3 (length-prefixed metadata + encrypted content)
- i18n: Spanish, English, Portuguese
- Themes: Dark and light mode
- License: MIT
- Author: José Carrillo (@carrilloapps)
- Repository: https://github.com/carrilloapps/zefer
- Live: https://zefer.carrillo.app

## What Zefer does NOT do

- Does not store any data on servers
- Does not use cookies, trackers, or analytics
- Does not collect personal information
- Does not have user accounts
- Does not transmit passphrases or encryption keys
- Cannot recover lost passphrases
